channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Texas’ order to ask hospital patients’ citizenship status renews focus on the state’s large uninsured population
item.description: On Nov. 1, hospitals will begin asking patients their citizenship status. But data suggests uninsured citizens, not immigrants, cost Texas hospitals more.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Terri Langford, KPRC 2

item.title: Biden making quick trip to Germany before US election to discuss Ukraine and democracy with allies
item.description: President Joe Biden has long believed diplomacy is about personal relationships — and he’ll spend Friday in Berlin with German Chancellor Olaf Scholz as his time in office is on the cusp of ending.
item.dc:creator: Josh Boak, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Vulnerable Dem senator tied to 'radical groups' working to close massive power plant in swing state
item.description: Vulnerable Democratic Sen. Sherrod Brown has partnered with multiple groups that are attempting to shut down the largest coal plant in the state of Ohio.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: FLASHBACK: Reaction to Harris' plagiarism story much more benign than for Biden during '88 election
item.description: Reactions to plagiarism accusations levied against Kamala Harris seem more benign than what Joe Biden faced in his presidential bid in 1988 after being accused of the same thing.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel’s UN ambassador: Response to Iran will be ‘very painful'
item.description: The world awaits Israel’s response to Iran’s missile attacks on Oct. 1, and the nation’s U.N. ambassador, Danny Danon, promised it will be "very painful" in order to deter Iran from attacking again.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump rips Harris on illegal immigration during Latino voter town hall: Hispanic voters 'most against it'
item.description: Former President Donald Trump joined Univison for a town hall on Wednesday in Miami to speak directly with undecided Latino voters and their top election concerns.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: As women seek open congressional seats in Maryland, reproductive rights are front and center
item.description: FREDERICK, Md. (AP) — In an election where the future of reproductive rights is on the ballot in Maryland and elsewhere across the country, the state’s all-male congressional delegation stands to gain an influx of women.
item.dc:creator: LEA SKENE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: With time running out, Harris keeps focus on 'blue wall' states
item.description: MILWAUKEE (AP) — With three weeks left in the presidential campaign, Democrat Kamala Harris is spending most of her days trying to shore up support in the “blue wall” states of Pennsylvania, Michigan and Wisconsin as she tries to avoid a repeat of Hillary Clinton’s collapse there eight years ago.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN and WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The ballot’s mailed but you die before Election Day. Now what?
item.description: Some states allow them to be counted, and some don't. But most states have never addressed the question.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Vance says 'no' when asked whether Trump lost 2020 election
item.description: In the months since he became Donald Trump’s running mate, Ohio Sen. JD Vance has repeatedly deflected questions about whether the Republican presidential nominee lost the 2020 election, saying he was focused on the future.
item.dc:creator: Thomas Beaumont, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Some Kern County voters say ballots are missing; election officials say it's not a concern
item.description: Once your ballot arrives in the mail, you can fill it out and then send it back by mail or drop it off at a drop box.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Huh, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Harris interview on Fox gets testy — but also gives her a do-over
item.description: The interview was the vice president's first formal sit-down with Fox. She faced questions on immigration, and was given a chance to answer again what she would do differently from President Biden.
item.dc:creator: Tamara Keith, MPR News

item.title: Harris and Trump offer new details about policies and strategy in dueling interviews
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As the presidential race moves into its final weeks, Democrat Kamala Harris and Republican Donald Trump embarked on an interview blitz that offered new details about their policy priorities and their political strategies.
item.dc:creator: STEVE PEOPLES, COLLEEN LONG and NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Flurry of pre-election legal cases is now 'standardized' strategy, experts say
item.description: With Election Day less than three weeks away, at least 165 election-related lawsuits have been filed in the U.S. so far, largely in the seven key swing states.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Striking Boeing workers boo after Democratic Sen. Maria Cantwell criticizes Trump
item.description: Sen. Maria Cantwell, D-Wash., was booed after mentioning a housing shortage and blaming it on former President Trump during a union rally with striking Boeing workers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The 3rd Precinct keeps showing up in the GOP playbook. Minneapolis wants to turn the page
item.description: Speaking outside the former 3rd Precinct in Minneapolis this week, Republican vice presidential candidate JD Vance criticized Gov. Tim Walz for his response to civil unrest in 2020, painting a picture of a burned-down city with rampant crime.
item.dc:creator: Estelle Timar-Wilcox, MPR News

item.title: Fani Willis asks Georgia appeals court to restore 6 charges against Trump, co-defendants
item.description: Fulton County District Attorney Fani Willis is requesting a Georgia appeals court restore six of her original criminal counts against former President Trump and his co-defendants.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Minnesota Republican Party sues Hennepin County over election judges list
item.description: A state law requires each county in Minnesota select election judges from lists provided by the Republican and Democratic parties. A lawsuit claims Hennepin County violated state statute by not selecting judges from a GOP list.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Nebraska court allows voter registrations from people with felony convictions
item.description: Nebraskans with felony convictions may register to vote after the state's highest court ruled Wednesday that a top election official had no authority to deem a law restoring those rights unconstitutional.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: Trump would be the oldest person to become president. He's not sharing health details
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — If he wins next month's election, Donald Trump would be the oldest person in U.S. history to be elected president. Yet the 78-year-old Republican nominee refuses to disclose new details about his physical or mental well-being, breaking decades of precedent.
item.dc:creator: STEVE PEOPLES and LAURAN NEERGAARD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What is Prop 36? A look into ‘The Homelessness, Drug Addiction, and Theft Reduction Act’
item.description: (KRON) – San Francisco Mayor London Breed, the California District Attorneys Association, and the California State Sheriffs' Association are all in support of Proposition 36, an initiative aimed to reverse a previous proposition that supporters claim exacerbated theft and crime around California.
item.dc:creator: Hamza Fahmy, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Eric Adams welcomes new administration members amid wave of resignations
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Some new faces are joining Mayor Eric Adams' administration as it works to stabilize City Hall after a series of high-level exits. A new city schools chancellor and a new deputy mayor for public safety are taking the reins.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Harris is pressed on the concerns of Black voters in Charlamagne tha God town hall
item.description: Harris’ town hall with the influential radio host came at a crucial moment in the campaign, as polls show her struggling to cement the level of support she may need from Black men to win the White House.
item.dc:creator: Deepa Shivaram and Jason Breslow, MPR News

item.title: Hurricane misinformation stokes fears ahead of election
item.description: The flood of conspiracy theories and rumors following the severe weather in the South is sparking concerns that political figures’ embrace of misinformation is just the beginning as the general election draws closer.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota city says Trump campaign still owes more than $200,000 for July rally
item.description: Local officials say former President Donald Trump’s campaign still owes a central Minnesota city nearly $209,000 for services related to a July campaign rally. The Minnesota Star Tribune reports that the bill is for services beyond what St. Cloud normally provides.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Donald Trump addresses Economic Club of Chicago
item.description: CHICAGO — Former President Donald Trump is back in Chicago Tuesday. Trump addressed the Economic Club of Chicago at Fairmont Chicago, located at 200 North Columbus Drive, ahead of the Nov. 5 election.
item.dc:creator: Christine Flores, WGN 9

item.title: Geraldo Rivera endorses Harris, says Trump 'unfit'
item.description: NewsNation correspondent-at-large Geraldo Rivera said Monday that he's endorsing Kamala Harris for president.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Rep. Marie Gluesenkamp Perez, challenger Joe Kent face off in Washington 3rd District debate
item.description: One of the races that could determine the fate of Congress this election season happens to be the rematch race for Washington's 3rd District, and on Monday night, the candidates will debate on KOIN 6.
item.dc:creator: Michaela Bourgeois, NewsNation

item.title: NJ lawmakers' fight against teen vaping continues with new bill
item.description: TRENTON, N.J. (PIX11) -- The fight against youth vaping continues in the Garden State as some Trenton lawmakers are trying to pass a law prohibiting the delivery of electronic smoking devices to people under 21, requiring someone over 21 to sign off on the delivery.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vasil, PIX 11

item.title: Native Voters could swing Arizona. Both parties want their votes.
item.description: Both Republicans and Democrats are trying to marshal Native American voters in Arizona, which could prove decisive to winning the key state.
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo, MPR News

item.title: Tim Scott mulls run to lead Senate Republicans' campaign arm
item.description: Sen. Tim Scott (R-S.C.) is seriously considering making a run to become chair of the National Republican Senatorial Committee (NRSC) in 2026, sources told The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Eric Adams is 'working well through the chaos': Gov. Hochul
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Gov. Kathy Hochul offered support for embattled New York City Mayor Eric Adams during the Columbus Day Parade in Manhattan on Monday. "Told him to bring in new blood… he's doing that… working well through the chaos," she told PIX11 News.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11
